facts.text: Jerusalem’s earliest settlement dates back to the Bronze Age, founded on a hill above the Gihon Spring and named Jebus in the Bible.
The Al‑Aqsa Mosque, built on the Temple Mount by Umayyad Caliph Al‑Walid, commemorates the spot Muslims say Muhammad ascended to Heaven.
Ancient Egyptian tablets from the 14th BCE call Jerusalem Urusalim, likely meaning “City of Shalem,” after a Canaanite deity.
Jerusalem is one of the world’s oldest cities, with a continuous history spanning more than 5,000 years.
The Jerusalem Development Authority brands the city as a global tourism icon, blending five millennia of history with cultural events.
Founded in 1925, the Hebrew University of Jerusalem ranks among the world’s top 100 universities.
